description: Formulates the Chvátal-Sankoff constant as the zero-temperature limit of a partition function for a directed polymer in a random medium. Match states act as attractive potentials in a 2D grid, mapping sequence alignment to energy minimization.
analogical_connections: Aligning two binary strings is akin to a polymer folding in a lattice to maximize contact with a specific type of impurity.
implementation_hypothesis: Simulate the finite-temperature partition function of the directed polymer and anneal T to 0. Use replica tricks to compute the average free energy.
experiment_seed: Write a Metropolis-Hastings sampler for polymer paths in a binary grid and extrapolate the energy scaling as N grows.
experimental_result: Polymer free energy interpretation explored; no quantitative bound obtained.
